article_meta.title: By Ellie CookSenior Defense ReporterShareNewsweek is a Trust Project memberU.S

reasoning: Mostly solid: flags genuine author-prose spin and subjectivity without quoting errors, but includes a clear false positive ('semi-autonomous part of Denmark') and one debatable label choice for the international law claim.

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: All flagged text is from the author’s prose; no quoted or clearly attributed source language was flagged.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: Flagged 'a semi-autonomous part of Denmark'—a factual descriptor—as biased; the international-law item is also debatable but defensible to flag.
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: Captured the main biased phrasings ('yawning chasm', 'rambling', 'unfathomable until now'); little else clear was missed.
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: Labels generally fit, but 'violation of international law' is better categorized as Opinion-as-Fact or Omission of Attribution rather than Spin.
